settingsMenu.title: App settings
settingsMenu.subtitle: Adjust display, language, local source vault, and AI agent connection checks in one place.
settingsMenu.liveVerdictSetupMeta: .mcp.json and Codex config mean the server can be started.
settingsMenu.liveVerdictSessionMeta: Only tools/list plus query_ontology inside Codex/Claude proves connected.
settingsMenu.liveVerdictFallbackMeta: mcp-verify checks the local server, not agent attachment.
settingsMenu.setupReadyBody: Confirms the repo has .mcp.json and Codex config pointing at this vault. This is setup, not live session proof.
settingsMenu.directProofBody: The current Codex or Claude session must expose tools/list with 24 tools, index_project, and callable query_ontology.
settingsMenu.fallbackProofBody: mcp-verify proves the local server and vault are healthy when the current agent has not loaded the MCP tools yet.
settingsMenu.staleCacheBody: If the tool description still says 23 tools or query_ontology is missing, treat it as stale client cache or an agent reload issue.
settingsMenu.stateLadderBody: Use these states before claiming the agent is connected; setup, live inventory, restart, fallback, and disconnected are different outcomes.
settingsMenu.proofDecisionSetup: Config present only means the server can be started.
settingsMenu.proofDecisionInventory: tools/list with 24 tools proves the server inventory is current.
settingsMenu.proofDecisionSession: The current agent namespace must show query_ontology before you call it direct MCP proof.
settingsMenu.proofDecisionFallback: If the namespace is stale, use CLI fallback and reload the agent before claiming live proof.
settingsMenu.appearanceBody: Switch between light and dark display modes.
settingsMenu.vaultBodyLocal: Open the current local source vault to review files and ontology nodes.
settingsMenu.vaultBodyStatic: Use the macOS app or local source vault flow before selecting a writable folder.
settingsMenu.agentBody: Review MCP settings, tools/list proof, and graph DB gates on the connection and verification screen.
settingsMenu.mcpProofBody: Connection is proven in the agent session, not by this screen alone. Run these first calls after Codex or Claude sees the server.
settingsMenu.mcpProofDirectLabel: Direct MCP proof in the current agent session
settingsMenu.mcpProofDirectBody: Treat it as connected only when the current session can list and call these tools.
settingsMenu.mcpProofCallTools: 2. Confirm tools/list has 24 tools, index_project, and query_ontology
settingsMenu.mcpProofFallbackBody: This proves the local MCP server and vault, not that the current agent has attached to it.
settingsMenu.mcpProofStaleCache: If it still says 23 tools or query_ontology is missing, reload/restart the agent or refresh cached MCP tools
settingsMenu.mcpStateConnectedBody: tools/list shows 24 tools and query_ontology runs in this session.
settingsMenu.mcpStateSetupOnlyBody: .mcp.json or client settings exist, but no live tools/list proof yet.
settingsMenu.mcpStateRestartBody: Config exists but this session still shows stale tools; restart or refresh the agent.
settingsMenu.mcpStateCliFallbackBody: Run mcp-verify and keep working through CLI fallback while the client session is refreshed.
settingsMenu.mcpStateDisconnectedBody: No server config or no callable tools; repair setup before trusting handoff.
settingsMenu.projectIndexCli: CLI plan: node cli/src/index.mjs index [codebase-root] --vault docs/ontology --json --threshold 2
settingsMenu.projectIndexMeaningGate: Meaning gate: report the business/product domain and capability first, then cite code rows as implementation evidence.
settingsMenu.projectIndexEvidence: Business evidence: report meaningGate.businessOntology.evidence rows from README and docs/ontology before treating source folders as capabilities.
settingsMenu.projectIndexReview: Review queue: report meaningGate.implementationEvidence.reviewRequiredRows so humans can name folders that still lack product meaning.
settingsMenu.projectIndexApply: Write only after review: add --apply when the human accepts the candidate batch.
settingsMenu.clientProofBody: Most MCP clients separate setup from live proof. Check both the configuration surface and the current session's tool inventory.
settingsMenu.clientCodexBody: Run codex mcp list, then confirm the tool namespace exposes list_kinds, validate_vault, and query_ontology in this session.
settingsMenu.clientClaudeBody: Use Settings or Developer logs for server status, then start a fresh session and confirm tools/list plus first calls.
settingsMenu.clientCursorVsCodeBody: Check the MCP settings file or integrations panel, then verify Agent mode can browse and enable the server tools.
settingsMenu.clientInspectorBody: Use the connection pane and Tools tab to list schemas and run a sample call before trusting a server change.

landing.subtitle: Install the app, pick a local vault folder, and start. Map features, modules, and dependencies as markdown files; browse them as a tree or graph; let AI agents work on the same files.
landing.topologyCaption: TOPOLOGY · {nodes, plural, one {# PROJECT} other {# PROJECTS}} · {relations, plural, one {# RELATION} other {# RELATIONS}}
landing.step1Title: Write a markdown file per piece
landing.step1Body: One file per project / feature / module. Add a name and a few links in the header — that's a node, those are relations. AI agents (Claude Code, etc.) read and edit the same files.
landing.step2Body: "Who depends on this feature?" "What breaks if I change it?" — the answer is one click away instead of a search across files.
landing.step3Body: Browse as a tree, navigate as a graph, edit on a canvas. Distribution charts, dependency chains, hub nodes — all from the same folder.

openSource.title: Built in public for developers who use AI coding agents.
openSource.body: Ontology Atlas is the user-facing app for the MIT-licensed ontology-atlas project by stark (Jinan Choi / 최진안). It exists because AI coding agents keep forgetting the long-term shape of a codebase. The project keeps that shape in markdown frontmatter, inside your repo, so Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and humans can share one memory.
openSource.stackValue: Next.js · TypeScript · Sigma.js · MCP · static hosting

download.title: Install once. Work from your local vault.
download.subtitle: The desktop app opens your markdown vault directly, remembers recent vaults, and keeps the hosted site out of your daily ontology workflow.
download.releaseAvailabilityNote: If no macOS DMG is visible yet, the direct-download app release is still waiting on PR review, version alignment, Developer ID signing/notarization, or the v0.1.0 GitHub Release.
download.releaseStatusTitle: Before the first release is fully available
download.releaseStatusPr: The desktop release workflow must be merged to main before v0.1.0 can ship.
download.releaseStatusVersion: The v0.1.0 tag must match package.json, Tauri, and Cargo metadata.
download.releaseStatusSecrets: Apple Developer ID signing/notarization secrets are required for direct-download DMGs, not Mac App Store submission.
download.releaseStatusRelease: The v0.1.0 GitHub Release is the source of truth for verified Apple Silicon and Intel DMGs.
download.releaseStatusHosted: Separately, Firebase Hosting must deploy the promo/download site so /ko/download/ is live after the app release.
download.releaseStatusAuditBody: Run this before waiting on CI: it writes owner-grouped release blockers to JSON and a reviewer checklist so the app release and hosted download gates stay separate.
download.proofSigned: Release gate requires Developer ID signing
download.proofChecksum: Apple Silicon + Intel checksums are verified
download.step1Body: Use the latest stable GitHub Release, like an Obsidian-style direct download. Choose the aarch64 DMG for Apple Silicon Macs, or the x64 DMG for Intel Macs. Public builds are published only after signing, notarization, and checksum verification.
download.step2Body: Open the DMG, copy the app into Applications, then launch Ontology Atlas like any local macOS app.
download.step3Body: The app starts with a vault setup screen, then opens the native folder picker when you choose a local folder. Recent local vaults reopen without returning to the website.
download.step4Body: Use Settings → MCP/Agents to copy the setup gate, then verify that Codex, Claude Code, or Cursor reads and writes the same vault over MCP with CLI fallback.
download.releaseGateNote: The hosted site does not open or edit vault folders. Daily ontology work starts inside the installed app after the release asset passes the macOS signing, notarization, and checksum gates.

onboarding.title: Map your codebase together
onboarding.intro: A visual map of *features, relationships, and roles*. Draw it on the canvas — your AI agent (Claude Code, Cursor, …) reads and writes the same `.md` over MCP.
onboarding.stepPaletteStrong: Click a kind on the left (or press P/D/C/E)
onboarding.stepPaletteBody: — Project · Domain · Capability · Element. Hit the matching key to drop one without leaving the canvas.
onboarding.stepConnectStrong: Drag from a node's edge handle
onboarding.stepConnectBody: and drop on another node — the two get a *relation (depends / contains / describes)* between them. Vault↔vault relations open a write preview and preflight first; choose the relation key, then save. If either endpoint is unsaved (amber), click the **Save** chip on the edge to persist both at once.
onboarding.stepSaveBodyDownload: — straight to disk as `.md` when a vault is open. In the hosted browser this canvas is read-only; install the macOS app for writable local work. AI agents read and write the same map over MCP.

insights.title: Ask the graph, then verify the evidence
insights.subtitle: Check hubs, paths, impact, and ownership questions against the same local ontology. Before using answers in plans, campaigns, code changes, or AI-agent handoff, close the evidence with MCP/CLI proof and runtime gates.
insights.titleInfoAriaLabel: Explain the question and evidence surface
insights.questionPresetsTitle: Pick a role to ask the same graph for evidence.
insights.questionPresetsBody: Planning, marketing, leadership, development, and agent work start from different questions over the same ontology.

planning.title: Verify vocabulary boundaries before scope changes
planning.body: Start with hubs, domain coupling, and open ownership questions so a plan uses the same graph vocabulary as implementation.

marketing.title: Ground claims in verified capabilities
marketing.body: Use capability rows, relation examples, and copied graph handoffs before turning ontology language into campaign copy.

leadership.title: Read ownership and change impact first
leadership.body: Use the domain matrix, blast-radius handoffs, and health gate before treating graph metrics as a decision record.

developer.title: Trace implementation proof before writing
developer.body: If the graph evidence is enough, continue into Save/edit with the same reader context instead of starting from a blank canvas.

agent.title: Run MCP and CLI checks on the same graph
agent.body: Start with agent_brief, graph DB query contracts, and runtime gates before proposing code or ontology writes.

insights.emptyTitleBefore: Your ontology is empty. Start from the
insights.queryCockpitTitle: Ask small questions before trusting rows
insights.queryCockpitBody: Before interpreting hubs, coupling, paths, or impact, start with bounded graph questions. Node scans, edge scans, domain matrix, and path evidence stay tied to the same markdown vault.
insights.queryCockpitNextStepBody: Copy the CLI pack for terminal fallback, then run the runtime gate before treating scan rows as evidence.
insights.queryCockpitBusinessLaneBody: Answer the outcome, business boundary, capability claim, and implementation evidence questions before citing paths or APIs.
insights.queryCockpitBusinessOutcomeAcceptance: Accept if outcome + graph pressure + changed decision are all named.
insights.queryCockpitBusinessBoundaryAcceptance: Accept if boundary + match totals + coupling evidence are all named.
insights.queryCockpitBusinessClaimAcceptance: Accept if the human capability claim appears before implementation proof.
insights.queryCockpitBusinessEvidenceAcceptance: Accept if proof rows include followUp and a proves/disproves/needs review verdict.
insights.queryCockpitCompactSummary: Readiness {readiness} · Pack {pack} scans · MCP {mcp} calls · CLI {cli} fallbacks · Runtime gate {runtime} checks
insights.queryCockpitLiveGraphBody: Graph facts computed from the current graph manifest right now.
insights.queryCockpitLiveHealthBody: {unknown} unresolved stubs · {orphans} orphans. Zero blockers means scan rows are ready to narrow.
insights.queryCockpitLiveTraversalBody: Hubs and average degree make path, blast radius, and domain matrix queries useful.
insights.queryCockpitEvidencePlanBody: Run query_plan first for costly scans and traversal so cost and bounds are explicit.
insights.queryCockpitEvidenceScanBody: Run match_nodes, match_edges, domain_matrix, and path queries over the same local markdown graph.
insights.queryCockpitEvidenceFollowUpBody: Scan rows are candidates. Narrow them with node_profile, match_edges, blast_radius, and explain_relation.
insights.queryCockpitEvidenceProofBody: Close the evidence before writing with relation_check, all_paths evidence, and the sync gate.
insights.queryCockpitRunContractsAriaLabel: Evidence contracts for the run order
insights.queryCockpitRunDecisionRule: Treat scan rows and paths as candidates until the contract is reported; defer decisions when evidence.pathsComplete is false.
insights.queryCockpitScanContractBody: Before using match_nodes or match_edges as evidence, report totalMatches, limited, row count, and followUp.
insights.queryCockpitPathContractBody: For all_paths, report limit, searchBudget, expandedStates, exhaustive, totalPathsExact, and evidence.pathsComplete.
insights.queryCockpitProofBody: This JSON gate proves connectivity and fallback performance before scan commands run. Automation reads ok, performanceOk, failure count, and per-command timeout flags separately.
insights.queryCockpitRuntimeBody: The dogfood runtime gate then replays the same graph DB pack over the local markdown vault: health --json, focused blast_radius, scan follow-ups, relation_name_parity, pattern_walk/project_map containment, all_paths completeness, relation_check recommendation, and health check status all fail closed if their contract disappears.
insights.focusedProofRailBody: {kind} · {slug}. Jump to the node proof, browse the concept, or open Save/edit without scrolling past the global query cockpit.
insights.focusedProofBody: {kind} · {slug}. Save/edit and tree handoffs land here with node_profile, planned edge scans, relation parity, bounded path planning, relation_check, and the sync gate ready to copy.
insights.focusedProofSyncBody: {count} graph checks · health · cycles · growth_plan · maintenance_plan · validate_vault
insights.agentHandoffStartBody: Paste the readiness prompt into Claude Code or Codex first. If MCP is not connected yet, copy the CLI checks and run the same graph proof from Terminal.
insights.agentDogfoodRepoBody: When building this app, open the docs/ontology folder as the source vault, then run the same vault checks from Terminal after code changes.
insights.agentTerminalFallbackSubtitle: If the MCP connector is not registered yet, run these local CLI checks against the same vault.

agentNextAction.resolveUnknown: Resolve unknown/stub nodes with get_concept, find_evidence, then patch_concept or merge_concepts.
agentNextAction.addConcepts: Bootstrap the shape: add at least one domain, capability, and element before expecting useful graph answers.
agentNextAction.linkOrphans: Attach unconnected nodes with contains/domain/capabilities/elements relations so traversal has a spine.
agentNextAction.addRelations: Add real depends_on/relates/describes edges; sparse graphs make path and impact queries weak.
agentNextAction.inspectHubs: Start with workspace_brief, node_profile, and blast_radius on the suggested hub slugs.
agentNextAction.syncAfterChanges: After code changes, sync docs/ontology before finishing so Claude Code and Codex see the same graph.

insights.collaboratorInsightsSubtitle: A copyable overview for planning, marketing, and domain review that keeps shared vocabulary tied to the same local ontology graph.
insights.collaboratorVocabularyReuseAction: align naming, owner, and reuse context before external handoff
insights.collaboratorFocusAlignVocabulary: Align naming around the top hubs before teams reuse this vocabulary in plans, docs, or campaigns.
insights.collaboratorFocusTraceImpact: Trace cross-domain impact before changing scope, messaging, ownership, or implementation boundaries.
insights.collaboratorFocusResolveOrphans: Resolve open ownership questions first; unconnected concepts are not ready to become shared team language.
insights.collaboratorDecisionAlignExpected: Approve reused terms, rename unclear hubs, and confirm the owner for shared vocabulary.
insights.collaboratorDecisionAlignNextStep: Open hub handoffs in Ontology, Topology, or Save/edit before copying terms into plans or campaigns.
insights.collaboratorDecisionImpactExpected: Confirm affected domains, messaging promises, and scope boundaries before changing vocabulary.
insights.collaboratorDecisionImpactNextStep: Open the Topology Path handoff and replay the domain-matrix CLI/MCP checks.
insights.collaboratorDecisionOrphanOwner: Domain owner for each open concept
insights.collaboratorDecisionOrphanExpected: Assign a container, merge a duplicate, rename unclear language, or delete the orphan.
insights.collaboratorDecisionOrphanNextStep: Open the Save/edit or Topology health handoff, then run the agent ownership checks before writing.
insights.collaboratorReaderPlanningQuestion: Are vocabulary boundaries reusable for the next scope change?
insights.collaboratorReaderMarketingQuestion: Which capability evidence can be used in external claims?
insights.collaboratorReaderLeadershipQuestion: Who owns the affected domains and impact boundary?
insights.collaboratorReaderDeveloperQuestion: Which implementation proof should change with the capability?
insights.collaboratorReaderAgentQuestion: Which MCP or CLI proof should be replayed before writing?
insights.collaboratorQuestionAlignReuse: Which terms should planning, docs, or campaigns reuse exactly?
insights.collaboratorQuestionAlignRename: Which high-signal term needs a clearer name before handoff?
insights.collaboratorQuestionAlignOwner: Which shared vocabulary hub needs an explicit owner?
insights.collaboratorQuestionImpactDomains: Which domains are affected if this vocabulary or scope changes?
insights.collaboratorQuestionImpactMessaging: Which message, ownership, or roadmap promise needs review?
insights.collaboratorQuestionImpactBoundary: Which implementation boundary should not move with the wording?
insights.collaboratorQuestionOrphanContainer: Which domain, capability, or project should contain it?
insights.collaboratorQuestionOrphanAction: Should the concept be linked, merged, renamed, or deleted?
insights.agentRecipesSubtitle: First MCP graph calls to run from Claude Code or Codex, with exact JSON arguments.
insights.agentGraphDbPackSubtitle: Copy graph-database-style scans that keep local markdown evidence bounded, planned, and follow-up driven.
insights.agentGraphDbSelfCheckHint: The CLI pack starts with this JSON gate to verify connection and fallback timing, and the dogfood runtime passes the health gate before scan commands run.
insights.agentGraphDbModeCliDesc: Use validation, summaries, graph scans, paths, and graph DB packs from Terminal without MCP.
insights.agentGraphDbModeMcpDesc: Let Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor call local tools with structured repair fields and write guardrails.
insights.agentGraphDbModePackDesc: Explore with planned node/edge scans, domain matrix, paths, and relation explanations without a DB server.
insights.agentGraphDbModeGateDesc: Run the JSON fallback check before edits and read ok separately from performanceOk.
insights.agentGraphDbFacetsPrompt: Read kind, domain, degree, relation, and schema-pattern buckets before choosing a narrower graph query.
insights.agentGraphDbNodeScanPrompt: Find high-degree capabilities before onboarding, slicing work, or choosing a refactor entrypoint.
insights.agentGraphDbEdgeScanPrompt: Inspect dependency edges with query_plan first, then follow edge evidence before changing boundaries.
insights.agentGraphDbDomainCouplingPrompt: Read coupling and hub ranking together so domain-level architecture work starts from measured pressure.
insights.agentGraphDbPathEvidencePrompt: Plan and bound path enumeration, then explain the relation before treating a path as proof.
insights.agentGraphDbBusinessQuestionsPrompt: Answer outcome, domain boundary, capability claim, and implementation evidence questions with graph queries instead of source-path guesses.
insights.agentGraphDbPackStepsLabel: Graph DB query pack MCP calls
insights.agentHandoffSubtitle: Copy this into a fresh Claude Code or Codex session to make ontology reads, health gates, query planning, and vault sync the default workflow.
insights.agentTraversalContractBudget: Report limit, searchBudget, expandedStates, exhaustive, truncatedByBudget, totalPathsExact, evidence.status, evidence.reason, and evidence.pathsComplete.
insights.agentTraversalContractPartial: Treat paths as partial evidence unless evidence.pathsComplete is true; when evidence.status is partial, follow suggestedQuery or saferQuery.
insights.agentTraversalContractProof: Do not use one returned path as proof when the query is limited, budget-truncated, or pathsComplete is false.
insights.agentScanContractTotals: Report totalMatches, limited, row count, and followUp details before treating scan rows as evidence.
insights.agentScanContractNodeFollowUp: For match_nodes, run followUp calls such as node_profile, match_edges, and blast_radius before onboarding or refactor decisions.
insights.agentScanContractEdgeFollowUp: For match_edges, run followUp calls such as explain_relation, path, and relation_check before write, refactor, or coupling evidence.
insights.agentTraversalStrategySubtitle: Plan before enumerating paths, treat bounded all_paths as evidence, then cross-check project/domain containment.
insights.agentTraversalStrategyPlanPrompt: Run query_plan(all_paths) first so high-degree hubs and broad bounds are narrowed before traversal.
insights.agentTraversalStrategyBoundedPrompt: Run all_paths only with maxHops, relation types, limit, and searchBudget, then report evidence completeness.
insights.agentTraversalStrategyContainmentPrompt: Compare pattern_walk and project_map before changing ownership, domain boundaries, or relation direction.
insights.agentRunOrderSubtitle: A compact first-contact sequence with MCP calls and CLI fallback for agent sessions.
insights.agentEntrypointsSubtitle: Use these high-degree nodes when a graph query needs a concrete slug instead of a placeholder.
insights.agentPlaybooksSubtitle: Intent-first graph workflows that bundle several MCP calls for common agent tasks.
insights.agentPlaybookRefactorPrompt: Profile a target slug, plan cost, inspect incoming blast radius and path evidence, then preflight relation changes.
insights.agentPlaybookOnboardingPrompt: Build a first mental map from workspace health, domain coupling, and one concrete hub profile.
insights.agentPlaybookCouplingPrompt: Check health, domain coupling, central nodes, and dependency edges before changing boundaries.
insights.agentPlaybookTraversalPrompt: Use schema, all_paths, pattern_walk, and project_map when an answer needs graph-query evidence instead of a single route.
insights.agentGuardrailsSubtitle: Read-first MCP preflights for the vault writes that usually cause stale edges, duplicate relations, or broken backlinks.
insights.agentRelationDecisionSubtitle: Use the decision field before any add_relation write. This keeps agent mutations explainable instead of raw graph edits.
insights.agentRelationDecisionSkipExisting: Exact edge already exists. Do not add another copy.
insights.agentRelationDecisionReviewInverse: Reverse edge exists. Check direction before writing.
insights.agentRelationDecisionSafeToAdd: Schema pattern is familiar. Add only after path evidence still supports it.
insights.agentRelationDecisionReviewNewSchema: New schema pattern. Pause and explain why this relation type belongs here.
insights.agentGuardrailRelationPrompt: Run relation_check and path first, then follow recommendation.decision so the edge is not duplicated, inverted, or already explained by a better relation.
insights.agentGuardrailRenamePrompt: Inspect backlinks and the local node profile before rewriting slugs that other concepts cite.
insights.agentGuardrailSyncPrompt: Gate the resulting graph with the 14-check runtime graph DB pack, health, cycles, growth_plan, maintenance_plan, and validate_vault before handing work back to another agent.
insights.agentRecipePromptAgentBrief: Start here when another agent needs the graph state, entrypoints, playbooks, and read-first write policy in one response.
insights.agentRecipePromptWorkspaceBrief: Start here for status, hotspots, health checks, and next actions.
insights.agentRecipePromptQueryPlan: Ask for cost, limits, and indexes before running a heavier graph query.
insights.agentRecipePromptHealth: Gate the vault before writes: unresolved refs, cycles, components, recommendations.
insights.agentRecipePromptComponents: Find disconnected graph islands before trusting traversal or onboarding maps.
insights.agentRecipePromptCycles: Check directed depends_on cycles and use the next cycle evidence loop before repair.
insights.agentRecipePromptTopologicalOrder: Read prerequisite-first dependency layers before sequencing refactors or onboarding.
insights.agentRecipePromptGrowthPlan: Review growth candidates and preflight recommended relations before writing to the vault.
insights.agentRecipePromptMaintenancePlan: Open the ordered maintenance queue and narrow by phase, severity, or kind before acting.
insights.agentRecipePromptNodeProfile: Inspect one hub deeply: aliases, incoming/outgoing edges, containment, lineage, and local risk.
insights.agentRecipePromptPath: Prove how two important nodes are connected before changing a boundary or relation.
insights.agentRecipePromptExplainRelation: Explain why two nodes are related with direct edges, shortest path, domain context, and common neighbors.
insights.agentRecipePromptSimilarNodes: Before creating or renaming a concept, check likely duplicates and shared-neighbor signals.
insights.agentRecipePromptRelationCheck: Preflight a proposed add_relation: inspect recommendation.decision, inverseEdges, schema familiarity, and the exact add_relation args to use.
insights.agentRecipePromptBlastRadius: Use before refactors to inspect incoming impact and dependency risk for a slug.
insights.agentRecipePromptDomainMatrix: Compare domain coupling and find where the ontology is becoming too tangled.
insights.agentRecipePromptAllPaths: Enumerate bounded simple paths between two important slugs before deciding which relationship actually explains the connection.
insights.agentRecipePromptPatternWalk: Run an explicit relation-sequence traversal such as project → domains → capabilities when hierarchy matters.
insights.surfaceTabProofDesc: Separate direct MCP proof from CLI fallback proof before asking for hubs, paths, or impact evidence.
insights.surfaceTabCollaborationDesc: Keep reviewer-facing decisions, vocabulary, and change impact separate from agent runbooks.
insights.surfaceTabAgentDesc: Collect the first MCP calls, CLI fallbacks, and write guardrails for Claude Code and Codex.
insights.surfaceTabCensusDesc: Inspect what is in the graph: concept kinds, relation kinds, and domain coupling.
insights.bandProofDesc: Check whether answers from this graph are safe for people or agents to trust, then keep the MCP / CLI calls that reproduce the same evidence.
insights.sessionProofDirectBody: The live Claude Code or Codex session must expose tools/list with 24 tools, index_project, and callable query_ontology.
insights.sessionProofFallbackBody: mcp-verify proves the local server and vault are healthy, but does not prove this agent session loaded the tools.
insights.sessionProofCacheBody: If tools/list still shows 23 tools or query_ontology is missing, reload or restart the agent and refresh cached MCP tools.
insights.bandCensusDesc: Structural distribution by the numbers: node kinds, relation types, and domain coupling. Describes the vault, not its agent-readiness.
insights.projectPanelSubtitle: {count} projects total — domain · capability · element sums, sorted by size
insights.edgeTypePanelSubtitle: {count} relations total — split by semantic relation type
insights.domainCouplingReproduce: Re-run this semantic matrix from Claude Code, Codex, or a terminal with the same relation filters.
insights.domainCouplingEmpty: No cross-domain links yet. Domain boundaries are clean in the current graph.
insights.hubsEmpty: No connected nodes yet. Link nodes in Save/edit and the most-connected ones (hubs) will surface here.
insights.orphansPanelSubtitle: {count} nodes not hanging from the tree
insights.orphansEmpty: Every node is connected to the tree.
insights.orphansRepairPacketInspectNode: Inspect the node in topology health mode.
insights.orphansRepairPacketChooseOwner: Choose the nearest domain, capability, or project owner.
insights.orphansRepairPacketSyncGate: Run the post-change sync gate before handing the graph to another agent.

agentSetup.validationGateDescUnknown: Run vault validation or the CLI validate gate before handing this vault to an agent.

agentSetup.proofContractJsonGateDesc: agent-brief --verify-fallbacks --json reports ok and performanceOk before edits.
agentSetup.proofContractGraphBriefsDesc: workspace-brief and agent-brief --graph-db-pack describe the same local vault before writes.

agentSetup.copyFirstContactProofTitle: Copy the minimal setup gate, workspace-brief, agent-brief, graph DB pack, and mcp-verify proof sequence for a codebase-root agent session.
agentSetup.modeCliDesc: Run validation, summaries, graph scans, path/explain queries, and graph DB packs from Terminal without MCP.

agentSetup.modeGraphDesc: Explore with query plans, node/edge scans, domain matrix, paths, and relation explanations without a database server.

treeWarnings.title: {count} folded hierarchy relations
treeWarnings.body: Concept map draws one representative project -> domain -> capability -> element hierarchy path per concept. Multiple parents, cycles, and duplicate reach paths would make the tree harder to read, so those relations are folded out of the hierarchy and kept available in Verify graph and Edit relations.
treeWarnings.dialogDescription: The concept map is a readable representative path. This summary keeps graph relations that are not drawn as tree lines connected to Verify graph and Edit relations.
treeWarnings.rawHint: Raw notes are emitted by the deterministic tree builder. They are graph evidence for Query.

multiple-parent.label: Multiple parents
multiple-parent.hint: The tree shows the first parent only. Use Verify graph for the full relation set.

cycle.label: Cycle
cycle.hint: Cycle nodes are promoted to roots. Review relation direction in Edit relations.

self-parent.label: Self parent
self-parent.hint: Edges where a node parents itself are ignored by the tree.

duplicate.label: Duplicate reach
duplicate.hint: If one node is reached twice, the second tree position is skipped.

other.label: Other projection note
other.hint: Graph state outside the hierarchy projection.

workbench.graphDbLoopAction: Close the loop by proving scans, containment, paths, and impact against the local markdown graph.
